description: Analyze the architecture of the E. coli OmpF porin constriction zone. 1. Fetch PDB: 2OMF from the RCSB. 2. Isolate a single monomer (e.g., chain A) from the trimer. 3. Look down the pore axis and create a visualization showing the transverse electric field: color the key basic residues (Lys16, Arg42, Arg82, Arg132) blue, and the opposing acidic residues on the L3 loop (Asp113, Glu117) red. 4. Save a high-quality cross-section PNG to ~/PyMOL_Data/images/ompf_constriction_zone.png. 5. Measure the distance between the alpha-carbons (CA) of Arg82 and Asp113. 6. Write a structural analysis report to ~/PyMOL_Data/ompf_pore_report.txt identifying the main overarching structural fold (e.g., alpha-helix, beta-barrel, etc.), listing the specific basic and acidic residues mapped, and providing the measured Arg82-Asp113 distance in Angstroms.
